entry.title: Washington Post: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine live updates: Russian forces withdraw from Snake Island; NATO summit wraps up
  • Africa’s desperate hunger: Ukraine war pushes Somalia toward famine
  • Marcos Jr. sworn in as Philippine leader in resurgence of ex-dictator’s family
  • Proud Boys, the Base labeled far-right terrorist groups by New Zealand
  • Ben & Jerry’s back in West Bank settlements, East Jerusalem
  • Xi Jinping begins ‘victory lap’ in Hong Kong, as locals look on warily
  • U.S. to increase military presence in Europe
  • Did Putin inadvertently create a stronger NATO?
  • ‘I have no tears left’: Relatives learn loved ones died in Texas migrant tragedy
  • Erdogan gets political boost but few concessions with NATO deal
  • Those 300,000 high-readiness NATO troops? ‘Concept,’ not reality.
  • Companies in China are aiding Russia’s military, U.S. alleges
  • Sole surviving attacker in 2015 Paris terrorism rampage is convicted
  • How green became the color of abortion rights
  • Record heat wave cooks Japan, straining power grid
  • Brutal killing in India stirs fears of escalating religious violence
  • Potential tropical storm could douse northern South America and Aruba
  • Wives of Russian soldiers plead for their return from war
  • Pelosi receives Communion at Vatican after earlier U.S. bishop refusal
  • Putin’s ‘toxic masculinity’ to blame for Ukraine war, Boris Johnson says
  • Russian forces leave Snake Island, keep up eastern assault
  • Remains of Congo's independence hero interred amid honors
  • Mudslide in northeast India leaves 7 dead, 40 others missing
  • Sudan's internet access disrupted ahead of anti-coup rallies
  • Lawyer for convicted Paris attacker questions harsh sentence
  • Post Politics Now: Ketanji Brown Jackson to be sworn in as Supreme Court justice, making history
  • Liz Cheney says Republicans face a choice: Trump or the Constitution
  • Two Michigan prosecutors say they’ll enforce 1931 abortion ban despite injunction
  • Two workers fired for not attending company’s prayers, lawsuit says
  • Nats fan tracks pitchers’ poor starts, one stinker at a time
  • Yellowstone visitor gored in second bison attack in a month, park says
  • Black man paralyzed after abrupt stop in police van, attorneys say
  • Primaries: Mike Flood (R) is projected to win special House contest to replace Jeff Fortenberry (R-Neb.)
  • Post Politics Now: Breyer to retire Thursday from Supreme Court, clearing way for Jackson swearing-in
  • Jan. 6 committee subpoenas former White House counsel Pat Cipollone
  • Hutchinson testimony: Assessing the basis for aide’s explosive claims
  • Biden and NATO send Russia a defiant message
  • Hutchinson provided ‘nuggets’ for Justice’s criminal probe, experts say
  • Post-Roe, can states ban employer abortion-related aid? It’s not that simple.
  • How to fix the U.S. labor shortage? Provide decent child care.
  • Abortion is illegal for millions. Will Big Tech help prosecute it?
  • In ‘Hindsight,’ players explore memories and come to terms with grief
  • What I love about my home in Northern California’s wine country
  • Mount Vernon Triangle offers walkability to loads of attractions
  • Mass Surveillance in Schools Won’t Solve Mass Shootings
  • Can Saudi Aramco Meet Its Oil Production Promises?
  • Bitcoin’s Fair Value Is Tied to Gold and Tech Stocks
  • That EY Cheat Sheet Shouldn’t Work Anymore
  • How Duterte’s Time in Power Shook Up the Philippines
  • What Are the Policy Banks China Uses to Spur Economy?
  • A Basic Guide to ESG Investing and Why it Faces a Backlash: QuickTake
  • How Putin’s Hopes to Weaken NATO Did the Opposite
  • Abortion rights should be law, not a corporate perk
  • Putin wants to terrorize Ukraine into submission. It’s not working.
  • The fall of Roe is the culmination of the Democratic establishment’s failures
  • ‘Calm down,’ libs! A look back at select pieces of Roe commentary
  • The Supreme Court embraces originalism — and all its flaws
  • Putin is forcing Germans to get real about the military
  • Preparing for an ‘extreme’ abortion debate in Virginia
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 30 Jun, 2022

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 30 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-30 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Coronavirus outbreak in Hong Kong shifting from pandemic to endemic says government health adviser
  • Hong Kong protests: kitchen worker receives longest jail time so far for rioting and breaching mask ban during Wan Chai demonstration in 2019
  • CityU awards 49 local start-ups up to HK$1million each under HK Tech 300 Angel Fund
  • Sino Land sells all 238 flats at Lohas Park in three hours as buyers rush to get ahead of rising mortgage rates
  • BN(O) scheme: London mayor underlines support for Hong Kong people who move to the capital
  • Hong Kong police arrest alleged driver of vehicle seen travelling against traffic in dashcam video posted online
  • Chinese leader Xi Jinping hails ‘vitality’ of ‘one country, two systems’ in arrival speech in Hong Kong, endorses it as a ‘good system’
  • More than a tropical island: Phuket residents discover international school that offers world-recognised holistic ‘education in paradise’
  • China’s art-market rebound gives way to mood of uncertainty as economic challenges mount
  • In 1 word: how 25 people describe Hong Kong, a city close to their hearts
  • No 3 typhoon warning signal looms for Hong Kong as storm Chaba nears, with President Xi Jinping on visit for 25th anniversary of city’s return to Chinese rule
  • Hong Kong U-turns on tightening pollution rules for classic cars, keeping exemptions for vehicles aged 30 years or more
  • What Chinese President Xi Jinping told Hong Kong in 2017 and key messages he can be expected to deliver on 25th anniversary of handover
  • British MPs call for sanctions on Hong Kong, Chinese officials over breaches of handover agreement
  • Hong Kong readies for visit by Chinese President Xi Jinping for 25th handover anniversary celebrations
  • US-listed Full Truck Alliance and Kanzhun resume user registrations in sign of China’s cybersecurity probes nearing end
  • US firms in China praise quarantine cut, but coronavirus restrictions still weigh heavy on confidence
  • As China’s Communist Party grows to near 97 million, more members are younger and educated
  • Climate Change: Chinese offshore oil and gas giant eyes major clean energy output expansion to help nation reach environmental goals
  • JD.com extends access to Tencent’s WeChat for three years with US$220 million in stock
  • Chinese video marking Hong Kong’s handover anniversary shows PLA soldiers training with rapid-fire mortar
  • Webb telescope allows Nasa to reveal the deepest image ever taken of the universe
  • Chinese team implants brain sensor without cracking skull
  • China’s economic recovery clouded by zero-Covid even as manufacturing, services expand for first time in 4 months
  • Fewer billionaires in mainland China as ultra-wealthy feel pain of Beijing’s tech, property crackdowns, study says
  • US needs to do more to protect companies against state-sponsored cyberattacks, experts say
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping repeats call for tech self-reliance, innovative talent
  • Hong Kong University of Science and Technology gets approval for Guangzhou campus, in time for handover anniversary
  • US protectionism hits China hardest, and Chinese are more averse to trade because of it, study finds
  • Croatia’s China-built, EU-funded bridge to open over troubled waters
  • Beijing’s human rights policies drive unfavourable views of China, Pew survey finds
  • US sanctions 25 more Chinese entities, including firm that touted its technology could help Russia monitor Ukraine’s submarines and frogmen
  • Nato leaders say China is a ‘systemic challenge to Euro-Atlantic security’
  • ‘Giant genes’ shed light on how ‘alien’ crustacean has adapted to the deep sea
  • It’s a wrap for China’s Tianwen-1 Mars mission, but rover and orbiter still ready to work
  • China to press on with ‘zero Covid’, despite economic risks, Xi Jinping says
  • US trade sanctions on China an attempt to ‘frighten’ firms amid soaring Russian energy imports
  • China relaxes intercity travel restrictions by removing indication of Covid-19 risk on digital passes
  • New Pacific alliance PBP to counter China’s influence will likely grow to include more European nations, say analysts
  • Japan proposes joint custody amid rise in divorce, reluctance from legal sector
  • Coronavirus: South Korea approves first home-made Covid-19 vaccine
  • Marcos Jnr sworn in as Philippine president decades after father was ousted from power
  • How tropical islands in Indonesia and Thailand are the new working from home
  • North Korea’s woes overflow as Kim Jong-un battles flooding, Covid-19 outbreak
  • New Zealand Prime Minister Jacinda Ardern warns Nato of China’s rising assertiveness
  • Philippines’ food and energy crises, China oil deal to greet Marcos Jnr as he takes office
  • Singapore top court rejects Myanmar helper killer’s appeal against 30-year jail term
  • Russian troops withdraw from Ukraine’s Snake Island in ‘goodwill gesture’
  • Putin hits back at Johnson’s woman remarks, points to Thatcher’s Falklands war
  • Cassidy Hutchinson: The ex-Trump White House aide with a ‘smoking gun’
  • ‘High on meth’ truck driver pretended to be victim after Texas migrant deaths
  • ‘Noisy’ protester silenced outside UK parliament as police get new powers
  • How leaders mingle after Nato meetings: with fine food, art and music at Madrid’s famous Prado Museum
  • Google’s ‘fast track to surveillance’ sparks backlash from European consumer groups over ‘deceptive’ practices
  • Putin says invasion going ‘to plan’ as Russia fights to encircle Ukraine’s last eastern stronghold
  • Vladimir Putin says Russia will respond if Nato deploys troops in Finland and Sweden
  • R&B star R. Kelly sentenced to 30 years for sex crimes
  • Lone surviving attacker in Paris massacre guilty of murder, jailed for life
  • Forever Covid-19? Reinfections more likely as virus evolves, experts warn
  • Britain’s Queen Elizabeth meets Scottish leader Sturgeon amid new independence vote push
  • Nato invites Sweden and Finland to join, calls Russia a ‘direct threat’
  • G20 boycott over Putin would leave floor to Russia and China, UK PM Boris Johnson warns
  • Climate change: Only 11 per cent of listed firms globally on track to achieve key carbon-emissions goal, MSCI says
  • EU reaches agreement on crypto regulation requiring personal data collection on every transfer
  • Dynamic change may be in the past for an ageing Hong Kong amid greater mainland integration
  • Hong Kong stocks log best month since October on China outlook while Tencent, SenseTime tumble as insiders sell
  • NYSE-listed Chinese low-cost retailer Miniso targets US$116 million in secondary Hong Kong float
  • Carousell rides sustainability trend in Hong Kong, aims to make buying and selling of second-hand items ‘as frictionless as possible’
  • Alibaba’s cloud services unit launches carbon management tool for its global business customers to aid decarbonisation drive
  • Coal-rich, gas-poor China needs more carbon capture and energy storage tech for new power system, analysts say
  • Dethroning of China’s ‘live-streaming queen’ and ‘lipstick king’ is not doomsday for the sector
  • How 2022 is shaping up to be a year of redemption for Chinese equities
  • The intelligent enterprise is key to Hong Kong’s economic future
  • Shanghai reopening: as restaurants restart offering dine-in services, owners say any more lockdowns will be ‘a fatal blow’
  • Hong Kong skids to 10th among global IPO venues as funding falls to a 19-year low, but brokers hopeful about second half of 2022
  • African Fashion show at V&A in London marks museum’s push to reverse its exclusion and misrepresentation of continent’s creativity
  • Urban farmer who set out to make dining more sustainable in Hong Kong on bringing the ‘magical’ taste of fresh produce to the city
  • 5 healthy habits that are trending across Hong Kong in 2022
  • Chinese tourists missing out as global tourism resumes – will they ever return in numbers again?
  • Liposuction, nose job abroad: why people travel for cosmetic surgery and the factors to consider
  • Norah Jones on her 2002 debut album Come Away With Me that won six Grammys and sold 27 million copies: ‘It was a whirlwind!’
  • 5 new K-dramas to watch in July 2022: Netflix’s Remarriage and Desires, Kim Se-jeong in Today’s Webtoon, Big Mouth, and more
  • Meet Nico Santos, set to star in Marvel’s Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3: the Filipino-American LGBT actor was a part-time waiter before he rose to fame in NBC’s Superstore and Crazy Rich Asians
  • 5 Hong Kong celebrities who look amazing at age 60: Tony Leung, Stephen Chow, Rosamund Kwan, Pansy Ho and Alex To all hit the milestone birthday in 2022 – but aren’t showing it a bit
  • How Kenzo found a preppy, 70s groove at Paris Fashion Week: Nigo’s autumn/winter 2022 menswear collection mixed Scout’s scarves with African vibrancy and Asian crossover styles
  • 10 celebrity dads with mirror-image sons following in their footsteps: Will and Jaden Smith, Princes Charles and William, plus Tom and Colin Hanks all show star power runs in the genes
  • How did Lisa Marie Presley lose all the Elvis millions? She inherited the King of Rock ‘n’ Roll’s Graceland estate and was even married to Michael Jackson, but is now US$16 million in debt
  • Squid Game’s 11 richest cast members – net worths, ranked: from HoYeon Jung’s lucrative Adidas campaign, to Park Hae-soo’s new Money Heist Korea role and Gong Yoo’s Jeju villa
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

entry.title: Guardian: 30 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
